# Boss Raid Docs Documentation

> Mercenary orchestration, provider routing, and settlement

Boss Raid is a raid-oriented execution platform. Mercenary sanitizes tasks, routes work to fresh HTTP providers, evaluates submissions, and splits payout equally across successful providers only.

## Getting Started

- [Introduction](/docs/getting-started/introduction): Boss Raid is the platform.
- [Quick Start](/docs/getting-started/quick-start): Use this path if you want Boss Raid running locally with the fewest steps.
- [Local Development](/docs/getting-started/local-development): Boss Raid is a pnpm monorepo with app surfaces, shared packages, examples, scripts, and internal markdown docs in the main app repo.

## Platform

- [Product Model](/docs/platform/product-model): Boss Raid is a raid-oriented execution platform for sending one task to multiple providers and selecting valid outcomes through evaluation.
- [Architecture](/docs/platform/architecture): Boss Raid has four layers: API, orchestrator, providers, and shared packages.
- [Raid Lifecycle](/docs/platform/raid-lifecycle): Mercenary runs one state machine from request intake to settlement.
- [Providers](/docs/platform/providers): Providers are external HTTP workers.
- [Apps And Packages](/docs/platform/apps-and-packages): Boss Raid is organized as apps for execution surfaces and packages for shared platform logic.

## API Reference

- [Overview](/docs/api-reference/overview): The public HTTP API is implemented in the app repo under `apps/api/src/index.ts`.
- [Native Raid](/docs/api-reference/native-raid): `POST /v1/raid` is the native Boss Raid public action route.
- [Chat Completions](/docs/api-reference/chat-completions): `POST /v1/chat/completions` exists for text-first, OpenAI-compatible workflows.
- [Providers And Registry](/docs/api-reference/providers-and-registry): This section covers both provider callback routes and the public registry/discovery surface.
- [MCP Server](/docs/api-reference/mcp-server): Boss Raid ships an MCP adapter in `apps/mcp-server`.

## Operations

- [Runtime And Environment](/docs/operations/runtime-and-environment): This page lists the commands and env vars that matter most.
- [Persistence And State](/docs/operations/persistence-and-state): Boss Raid persists provider state and raid state so the API, ops surface, and settlement tooling can work against the same data.
- [Settlement And Contracts](/docs/operations/settlement-and-contracts): Boss Raid supports both file-based settlement records and an on-chain bootstrap path.
- [Troubleshooting](/docs/operations/troubleshooting): Boss Raid now fails fast when no fresh provider satisfies the request.

## Reference

- [Examples And Payloads](/docs/reference/examples-and-payloads): The Boss Raid app repo ships a small set of source-of-truth example payloads.
- [Current Limits](/docs/reference/current-limits): This page is intentionally blunt. These are the current constraints of the platform, not aspirational marketing.
- [UI Direction](/docs/reference/ui-direction): Boss Raid has two different UI surfaces and they should stay different.
- [Hackathon](/docs/reference/hackathon): Boss Raid.
- [Synthesis Registration](/docs/reference/synthesis-registration): This page captures the current registration decision and the required fields for the SYNTHESIS flow.
